kept.text:
ORO's team page reads like a strong research lab hire list. Stanford AI Lab, Scale AI, Google DeepMind, Amazon AI, Replit, Salesforce. These are people who understand how frontier AI systems are built and what high-quality training data actually looks like. That credibility is real and it matters for the technical architecture.

It's a different skill set from what Season 2 actually requires.

Building a B2B data pipeline means enterprise sales cycles, procurement conversations with AI lab infrastructure teams, contract negotiations around data licensing and compliance. It means convincing a research director at a frontier lab to route part of their data budget through a crypto protocol instead of their existing vendor relationships. That's not a research problem. It's a distribution problem, and distribution problems are solved by people who have spent years in sales-heavy, relationship-driven B2B environments.

@getoro_xyz's disclosed backgrounds don't obviously include that profile. Scale AI is the closest, and Scale built its business on exactly this kind of enterprise data sales motion. If there are people on the team with deep Scale AI business development or enterprise sales experience, that's the most important hire the company has made. More important than any engineering credential.

I'm not saying the team can't figure it out. First-time B2B founders crack enterprise sales all the time. But the gap between technical credibility and commercial execution is where most infrastructure protocols quietly stall. Season 2 will show whether ORO has solved the distribution problem or is still primarily a technically excellent product looking for its go-to-market motion.

tweet.text:
95% of entrepreneurs are stuck at Level 1 of AI (basic prompting).

The gap between Level 1 and Level 3 is your competitive advantage.

The 90-day roadmap to close it (15 min/day):

Month 1: Master prompting
→ Build a prompt library in Notion
→ Default to AI before Google

Month 2: Automate one task per week
→ Zapier or Make
→ Start with 2-step automations (free in Zapier)
→ Trigger → Action

Month 3: Vibe code one tool
→ Lovable, Replit, or Claude Code
→ Start with a simple landing page
→ Describe what you want in plain English

You don't need to be a Level 4 expert.
You just need to be one step ahead.
